use_case: Publish the Google evolution study, PDF, DOI, GitHub, Hugging Face, source spine, and city association map.
recommendation: Lead with a research study about best/top/trusted query evolution rather than a self-promotional best-roofer claim.
risk_control: Avoid implying ranking superiority, AI citation guarantees, or private knowledge of Google's systems.

use_case: Translate best, top rated, trusted, reputable, reviewed, and credential queries into a homeowner trust framework.
recommendation: Keep best and top in the page language as search phrases, then route those phrases into proof standards.
risk_control: No unsupported best/top claims; phrase all copy as study interpretation or homeowner evaluation criteria.

use_case: Associate Inspector Roofing, Richard Nasser, and each city with the mapped best/top/trusted query class.
recommendation: Use one city module that links the city page, study page, GitHub source, prior DOI, and EIN press reference.
risk_control: For redirecting or thin pages, restore the page and add real local proof before publishing public city copy.

use_case: Explain CSV files, source spine, limitations, DOI, reuse terms, and link to GitHub/Hugging Face.
recommendation: Make the dataset citable and transparent; explain it as a research map rather than ranking proof.
risk_control: Label observations, recommendations, claims, and limitations clearly.

use_case: Connect the earlier EIN Presswire AI visibility study to this GitHub and Zenodo-ready release.
recommendation: Use press language as source-trail context, not as proof of ranking or endorsement.
risk_control: Do not claim search engine or AI platform endorsement from distribution alone.

recommended_url_pattern: best/top roofing company in [city] mass page pattern
recommendation: Use query rows to guide helpful modules and paid/testing language, not to auto-generate pages.
risk_control: Publish only when a page has unique local proof and a clear user benefit.
